style_guide.art_style: Hand-painted gouache illustration with visible brushstrokes and rich, opaque layers.
style_guide.color_palette: Vivid, saturated colors like sapphire blue, teal green, coral orange, sunny yellow, and deep indigo, with bright white highlights.
style_guide.lighting: Warm, sparkling sunlight dappling the scene from the upper center, casting shimmering patterns below.

characters.name: Leo
characters.description: Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.

characters.name: Mia
characters.description: Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.

pages.narration: Imagine an underwater world bigger than any city, full of secrets! The ocean is so huge it covers over seventy percent of our planet. Can you feel the cool water and see the bright sunshine sparkling on the waves? In this top layer, the Sunlight Zone, tiny floating plants make food using sunlight, just like plants on land. It's bustling with life, like a giant watery playground.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache illustration, Leo (Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) standing on the sandy seabed in shallow, crystal clear water, looking up at the shimmering surface. Sunlight streams down in bright shafts, highlighting colourful small fish. The water is a brilliant turquoise, and the scene is full of energy and warmth. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: shallow sandy seabed in crystal clear water
visual.action: standing on the seabed, looking up at shimmering surface with sunlight streams and colorful small fish

pages.narration: But the ocean isn't just a sandy beach; it gets deeper and weirder! When you sink down, the water gets colder, and the light starts to fade into a mysterious blue. Welcome to the Twilight Zone, where crazy creatures make their own light. Did you know that ninety percent of animals here glow in the dark? It's called bioluminescence, and it helps them find food or scare away predators in the dim, mysterious deep.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache illustration, Leo (Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) within a small, glowing yellow submersible, looking out of a large viewport into deeper, murky water. The water is a deep, indigo blue, filled with strange, shimmering bioluminescent fish and jellyfish that flash in the gloom. The submersible casts a warm light on nearby rocks, but the surrounding space is dark and full of shadows. The mood is tense and curious, and the scene is lit by the creatures' light and the submersible's beam. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: small glowing yellow submersible in deep indigo water
visual.action: peering through viewport at strange glowing bioluminescent fish and jellyfish

pages.narration: Beyond the Twilight Zone, the ocean goes down even deeper, into absolute blackness. This is the Midnight Zone, where the pressure is intense and nothing that needs sunlight can survive. The deepest point in the entire ocean is the Mariana Trench, which is nearly seven miles deep, deeper than Mount Everest is tall! We can't go down there without special equipment, so we must make a big decision about our next adventure.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache illustration, Leo (Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) still in the submersible, looking at a split navigation panel showing two potential directions. One way leads to the dark, deep trench, with tiny lights representing creatures, while the other leads back up towards a vibrant coral reef. Leo points excitedly to one path, while Mia carefully considers. The submersible is still in the deep blue, and the mood is thoughtful and adventurous. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: submersible looking at split navigation panel in deep blue
visual.action: examining navigation panel with choices: deep trench and colorful coral reef, Leo points to one path

choice.question: Which ocean adventure will you choose?

option_a.label: Dive down to explore the Midnight Zone

pages.narration: Okay, get ready for extreme pressure and total darkness as we enter the Midnight Zone! Our submersible's searchlights are the only thing cutting through the inky black water, revealing some truly bizarre animals. See that strange anglerfish with its glowing light lure? These creatures are experts at surviving where there is no sunlight, and they have amazing adaptations to catch food.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache illustration, Leo (Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) peering through the submersible's viewport into the absolute pitch-blackness of the Midnight Zone. The submersible's powerful searchlight reveals a strange, spiky anglerfish with its glowing lure bobbing just outside, along with other unusual, blind deep-sea creatures. The water is a deep, almost black indigo, with only the bright cone of light illuminating the creatures and the rocky, barren seabed. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: submersible peering into pitch-black Midnight Zone with searchlights
visual.action: looking through viewport at strange spiky anglerfish and other blind deep-sea creatures in the light beam

pages.narration: Look closely at the strange chimneys rising from the deep-sea floor! These are hydrothermal vents, places where super-heated water, full of chemicals, shoots out from underneath the Earth's crust. It's so hot, yet these vents are surrounded by colonies of giant tubeworms and white crabs that have never seen the sun. They get all their energy from the chemicals in the water, not sunlight—pretty cool, huh?
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache illustration, Leo (Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) in the submersible, looking out at a field of towering, smoky hydrothermal vents on the barren seabed. Giant red-tipped tubeworms and white crabs cluster around the vents in the cold, deep blue water. The only light comes from the submersible's beam and some eerie chemical glow from the vents, making the scene feel otherworldly and mysterious. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: submersible looking out at field of smoky hydrothermal vents on barren seabed
visual.action: observing giant red-tipped tubeworms and white crabs clustered around vents in deep blue water with eerie light

option_b.label: Swim near a colorful Coral Reef

pages.narration: Wow, look at all the incredible colors! A coral reef is like a bustling underwater city, filled with more kinds of animals than almost anywhere else on Earth. But did you know that the actual coral isn't a rock, it's thousands of tiny animals called polyps living together? These polyps build the hard structure of the reef over thousands of years, creating a home for fish, sharks, and turtles.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache illustration, Leo (Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) snorkeling at the surface of a shallow, sunny coral reef, looking down at the incredible, vibrant colors. The water is bright turquoise and full of sunshine. Below them, massive brain coral, fan coral, and staghorn coral create a vibrant, complex structure, teeming with thousands of colourful small fish. The mood is joyful and amazed. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.action: snorkeling, looking down at massive brain, fan, staghorn corals with thousands of colorful small fish

pages.narration: See that funny little fish wiggling in the anemone? That's a clownfish, and it has a special relationship with the poisonous anemone that helps them both survive. The anemone protects the clownfish from predators, and in return, the clownfish cleans the anemone and scares away other fish. It's called symbiosis, which means two different kinds of creatures help each other out in the ocean.
pages.image_prompt: Hand-painted gouache illustration, Leo (Fair skin, curly brown hair, hazel eyes, small build. Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) floating just above the vibrant coral reef, looking closely at a specific section. Mia points to a bright orange clownfish peeking out from the wavy, pale purple tentacles of a sea anemone, and colourful damselfish swim nearby. The sunshine creates bright, dancing light patterns through the turquoise water onto the reef. The mood is curious and happy. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: above vibrant coral reef looking closely at specific section
visual.action: pointing at bright orange clownfish peeking from sea anemone, damselfish nearby, dancing light patterns on the reef

pages.narration: Whether you're exploring the deepest trench or swimming in a sunny reef, the ocean is a magnificent place that connects all living things on Earth. It helps control our climate, provides oxygen for us to breathe, and holds countless more secrets just waiting to be discovered. The ocean is full of wonder, and it's our job to explore it, learn about it, and keep it healthy for the future.

Wearing a bright yellow long-sleeved swim shirt with blue trim, green swim trunks, and a clear snorkeling mask perched on his forehead.) and Mia (Dark brown skin, black braided hair, big dark eyes, average build. Wearing a blue short-sleeved wet suit with orange panels, a black and pink snorkel around her neck, and a yellow waterproof wristwatch.) back on a beach during the warm, golden light of sunset, looking out at the sparkling, blue ocean horizon. They are smiling and holding onto their snorkeling gear, reflecting on their journey. The sky is filled with warm orange and purple hues, and the ocean water reflects the stunning colors, ending the adventure with a sense of wonder and connection. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.
